title: Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Bathu temples (बाथू मंदिर), known locally as Bathu ki ladi (बाथू की लड़ी), is a cluster of temples in the Kangra district of Indian state of Himachal Pradesh, with the main temple
overview.description: Bathu temples (बाथू मंदिर), known locally as Bathu ki ladi (बाथू की लड़ी), is a cluster of temples in the Kangra district of Indian state of Himachal Pradesh, with the main temple dedicated to goddess Parvati and Lord Shiva. These temples were submerged in Maharana Pratap Sagar, a reservoir created by Pong dam in the early 1970s. Since then, these temples are only accessible from May to June when the water level decreases.

topPlaces.name: Nagrota Surian
topPlaces.description: Nagrota Surian is a town in Nagrota Surian Mandal, Kangra District, in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Nagrota Surian is 45.2 km from the district headquarters Dharamshala.

topPlaces.name: Maharana Pratap Sagar
topPlaces.description: Maharana Pratap Sagar, also known as Pong Reservoir or Pong Dam Lake, is a large reservoir in Fatehpur, Jawali and Dehra tehsil of Kangra district of the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. It was created in 1975, by building the highest earthfill dam in India on the Beas River in the wetland zone of the Siwalik Hills.

topPlaces.name: Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Pong Dam Wild Life Sanctuary is a sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India. It covers 673 square kilometers.

topPlaces.name: Dhami State
topPlaces.description: Dhami was a Princely State situated 26 kilometres (16 mi) west of Shimla, India. Its capital was Halog and the state formed a part of the region known as the Punjab Hill States Agency during the British Raj period.

topPlaces.name: Dhan, Himachal Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Dhan is a village in the tehsil of Jawali, Himachal Pradesh in India. As of the 2011 census, the village had a population of 1,878 persons in 399 households.

topPlaces.name: Pong Dam
topPlaces.description: The Pong Dam, also known as the Beas Dam, is an earth-fill embankment dam on the Beas River in Khatiyar, Kangra district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India, just upstream of Talwara. The purpose of the dam is water storage for irrigation and hydroelectric power generation.

items.note: Nagrota Surian is a town in Nagrota Surian Mandal, Kangra District, in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Nagrota Surian is 45.2 km from the district headquarters Dharamshala.

Maharana Pratap Sagar, also known as Pong Reservoir or Pong Dam Lake, is a large reservoir in Fatehpur, Jawali and Dehra tehsil of Kangra district of the state of Himachal Pradesh in India. It was created in 1975, by building the highest earthfill dam in India on the Beas River in the wetland zone of the Siwalik Hills.
Pong Dam Wild Life Sanctuary is a sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India. It covers 673 square kilometers.

Dhami was a Princely State situated 26 kilometres (16 mi) west of Shimla, India. Its capital was Halog and the state formed a part of the region known as the Punjab Hill States Agency during the British Raj period.
Dhan is a village in the tehsil of Jawali, Himachal Pradesh in India. As of the 2011 census, the village had a population of 1,878 persons in 399 households.

The Pong Dam, also known as the Beas Dam, is an earth-fill embankment dam on the Beas River in Khatiyar, Kangra district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India, just upstream of Talwara. The purpose of the dam is water storage for irrigation and hydroelectric power generation.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (28 km) and the nearest railway station is Amritsar Junction (117 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: Stays in Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bathu Temples, Himachal Pradesh?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nagrota Surian, Maharana Pratap Sagar, Pong Dam Lake Wildlife Sanctuary, Dhami State, Dhan, Himachal Pradesh.
